bodyHTML:

Arc Flash Study

Carelabs provides affordable electrical arc flash analysis, investigation, and certification services to a variety of sectors. Certified specialists will examine the arc flash dangers, worker training needs, paperwork requirements, and PPE (Personal Protection Equipment) regulations to make sure your electrical safety program is up to date, compliant, and comprehensive. CareLabs may detect arc flash dangers early on and take the necessary action to lessen the impact of harmonic distortions in order to assure the equipment’s safety.  

Enquire
Contact Us

Arc flashes are investigated and analyzed using a program called ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program). The quantity of heat and light produced by an arc fault is known as an arc flash. This occurs when current flows via an air gap between two conductors. Arc flashes produce intense light and heat while ionizing the air around them. 35 000 °F or something like. Arc flash analysis is the study and evaluation of the electrical facility’s systems to determine the incident energy available for each electrical component. Arc flash studies increase workplace safety by specifying the safety criteria for licensed electricians working on equipment and circuits. Also included are enhanced electrical one-line diagrams, equipment locations, a short circuit, and coordination analysis.   

Arc Flash Research Objectives:
  • Calculating the damage caused by incident energy from an arc flash mishap.
  • Decreasing exposure to incident energy by modifying the protective equipment’s settings.
  • Establish arc flash safety guidelines.
  • Respect your nation’s laws and customs.
  • Identify the PPE that is worn while working.
Arc Flash's Harmful Effects:
  • Your skin, face, and eyes might be badly burned by arcs.
  • Inhaled gases and hot particles can seriously burn the throat and lungs inside.
  • Injuries caused by errant fragments and flying debris.

Arc flash investigations are essential for lowering risks and improving both the safety of electrical employees and the overall security of the company. Arc flash investigations assist in the evaluation of electrical danger levels and the implementation of appropriate safety measures to lower the risk of burns and accidents. Arc flash analyses are required for all utilities, manufacturing, industrial, and commercial operations that rely on crucial electrical parts such switches, panel boards, motor controllers, and electrical disconnects.   

The Benefits of Research on Arc Flash:
  • Enhanced human and mechanical safety.
  • Medical and legal costs have decreased.
  • Safety observance of legal requirements
  • Lessening of standards infractions and sanctions

The following are the steps in arc flash analysis:

  • Collecting information
  • Confirmation of location
  • The layout of a system.
  • Investigating arc flashes
CareLabs' workflow:

CareLabs offers excellent services and will perform any tasks required by the engineering business, neighborhood contractor, or construction crew.

Following are the steps in our working method:

  • Collecting information
  • ETAP is used for power system modelling.
  • Testing for short circuits.
  • Coordination of overcurrent protection devices
  • Investigating arc flashes
  • Arc flash evaluation
  • Keeping a ledger.
  • Techniques to lower the likelihood of an arc flash.
  • Diagrams with one line, big.
  • Arc flash markings are currently being included.
  • The completed text
Advantages of Employing CareLabs' Services:
  • Build electrical infrastructure that complies with norms and regulations.
  • Integrated modules for thorough short circuit, overcurrent coordination, device assessment, and arc flash evaluation are employed, in addition to safety devices and bus ratings.
  • Increasing workplace safety
  • Simple ideas for substitute designs
  • Increasing safety margins by employing user-defined arc fault limits.
  • Our computer-aided analysis might help you save time by automatically producing arc flash labels.
  • Reduce upkeep expenses at the expense of efficiency.

Crew members are deployed to various locations throughout our labs to ensure that our professionals are never far away in the event of a regular or urgent issue. CareLabs has established a solid reputation as a client-focused business with ISO 9001:2008 accreditation. Stockholm, Gothenburg, Malmo, and Uppsala are just a few of the major cities where we provide arc flash investigation and analysis services. Contact us right away for a free quote on our arc flash investigation and analysis.

title: Harmonic Study and Analysis
bodyHTML:

  • About Us

Harmonic Study and Analysis

CareLabs‘ electrical installation services include detailed harmonic analysis and research as well as safety inspections, testing, calibration, and certification. Trained experts will assess harmonic hazards, training programs, documentation, and personal protective equipment (PPE) to ensure that your electrical safety program is current, compliant, and comprehensive.

Enquire
Contact Us

CareLabs can detect harmonics early and take the necessary actions to reduce their influence, assuring the safety of the equipment. Harmonic voltages and currents can lead to electrical system inefficiency. They have the capability to reduce electricity quality and generate excessive heat. Harmonic voltage analysis is a mathematical technique for identifying possible resonances and degrees of harmonic distortion. Harmonic voltage analysis is a mathematical technique for determining potential resonances and harmonic distortion levels based on acquired data.    

Equipment for nonlinear electrical switching, such as AC variable frequency drives, DC drives, fluorescent lights, computers, and so on, introduce harmonic frequencies to the electrical system. Many pieces of machinery can malfunction when harmonic voltage/current levels are high. Harmonic analysis and research must be completed as quickly as feasible. 415 voltage systems currently have 5% harmonic limitations, with 4% odd harmonics and 2% even harmonics.   

Harmonic Distortion's Effects  
  • Entrusted with managing flowing current and high voltage.
  • Electronics are damaged and destroyed by excessive voltage distortion.
  • Due to a malfunction, the branch circuit breaker trips, and the buildup of internal energy in components results in component deterioration and a shorter lifespan.
  • Error measurements
  • Inside the wiring and distribution system, explosions, and flames
  • Power outages are caused by faulty generators.
  • A decrease in system power factor
  • Installation and utility costs are exorbitant.
  • Productivity is decreasing as a result of excessive travel and downtime.
  • Usage of stronger engines

Harmonic issues are extremely uncommon, but when they do arise, they have a substantial impact on the system’s dependability, power quality, and efficiency. Research and study of harmonics are so essential.

  • It provides protection against voltage and current harmonics.
  • Variation in frequency is minimal.
  • Uneven voltage and current
  • Improve power factor analysis and maintain a value around one.
  • Voltage variations are controlled.

Harmonic research and analysis can assist designers in avoiding harmonic-related obstacles and reducing the possibility of unexpected results by illuminating the likely causes and consequences of power quality issues.   

Harmonic Analysis and Research Workflow at CareLabs:
  • On the electrical one-line diagram, draw special attention to reachable nonlinear loads, capacitor banks, and lengthy medium voltage connections.
  • Locate the common point of connection, or PCC.
  • Determine which system buses in the building are susceptible to harmonic distortions.
  • assemble harmonics data for all nonlinear loads.
  • Obtain critical information about the Australian-allowed harmonic values, the maximum and minimum short circuit fault levels, and the selected PCC. Additionally crucial for understanding voltage and current harmonics is this data.
  • ETAP is used to do a harmonic analysis on the electrical network.
  • Determine the voltage and current wave distortion values for the bus and PCC, both collectively and individually.
  • An appropriate mitigation plan is created and implemented if harmonic distortion levels exceed the allowable limits.
  • Run the analysis again after adding the mitigation approach.
Advantages of CareLabs' Service

In cases when background harmonics have previously been identified, the Stage 2 values are greater.

  • Stage 2 permits changing the converter size based on fault level and converter type if the background is less than 25%.
  • If the background is between 25% and 75%, converter ratings of 50% of the quantity specified above are acceptable.
  • For the top backdrop, an extensive harmonic analysis is performed.

CareLabs has staff members stationed in various areas to make sure that our professionals are available for routine or urgent situations. CareLabs developed itself as an organization that has gained ISO 9001:2008 accreditation fast thanks to a substantial client base that has provided excellent feedback. Stockholm, Gothenburg, Malmo, and Uppsala are just a few of the significant Irish cities where we provide harmonic research and analysis services. Contact us right away to set up harmonic research and analysis in Sweden or to get a quote!

title: Motor Start Analysis
bodyHTML:
• Home
  • About Us
  • Our Services
    • Arc Flash Study
    • Harmonic Study and Analysis
    • Power Quality Analysis
    • Power System Study and Analysis
    • Motor Start Analysis
  • Contact Us

Motor Start Analysis

CareLabs‘ Motor start study and analysis service is available to businesses in Sweden. Our motor start analysis service’s primary purpose is to identify any odd occurrences that may occur during the start of a large motor. Before starting an electric motor, it is critical to do more than simply ensure that the motor is correctly connected to the main power supply.

Enquire
Contact Us

Frequent annoyance tripping, high nominal currents, and lights that flicker or fade are all signs of a malfunctioning electrical system. CareLabs professionals will come to your location to capture installation data for your motors from the past and current. The acquired data and manufacturer requirements are then employed in mathematical models to predict negative events and devise countermeasures.

Motor starting techniques may be investigated using static and dynamic models. The study of dynamic motor beginning circumstances, including but not limited to:

  • The load flow solution is modelled using the starting impedance of the electric motor.
  • During starting, the voltage drop approach is used to measure the current in a motor, whereas the short-circuit method is utilized in pre-fault short circuit circumstances.
  • When a precise dynamic model of the electric motor circuit and load-torque characteristics is available, the effect of motor starting may be computed using a traditional transient stability technique in combination with a dynamic model of the electric motor.
  • Instead of a comprehensive dynamic model of the electric motor, transient stability simulations can be employed.
Sweden Should Carry Out a Motor Start Study:

Electric motors are widely employed in today’s production processes, and their size is increasing at an exponential rate. If you switch on a large motor linked to the line, the electric motor, any surrounding loads, and even buses further away may be severely impacted. To start an electric motor, a terminal voltage of 80% or greater of the prescribed voltage is required. Because a motor’s load torque is proportional to the square of the terminal voltage, every change in voltage has an instantaneous influence on the motor’s load torque characteristic.   

If the voltage drop is significant enough, the strain on the operating machines may approach their “breakdown torque,” forcing them to slow down or stop completely. Other types of loads, such as electrical equipment, delicate control systems, lighting loads, and so on, are also affected by the voltage drop. As a result, if the beginning current is too high, all systems that service a constant load will demand additional current since these decreases terminal voltage. Furthermore, problematic low-voltage relays have been discovered.   

When Should I Perform a Motor Start Analysis?
  • Prior to or during the purchase of a large engine.
  • In the absence of a generator and if the KVA rating of the motor exceeds 30% of the base KVA rating of the supply transformer.
  • If the motor rating exceeds 10 to 15% of the generator’s KVA rating and the generator is the only source of power for the system, the generator must be replaced.
  • If a process in an industry needs the start of many motors at the same time, a motor start analysis may be required.
  • Voltage drop issues are exacerbated when starting a high-power motor, which is especially true for low-power systems. Understanding the origins of motors is essential for comprehending the output of tiny systems with generators.
Evaluating the Utility of Motor Start:

Motor starting experiments Find a motor or a method of starting it that has the proper speed and torque parameters for the project. Certain loads must be accelerated under strict supervision to avoid exceeding the torque constraints of the equipment. A motor start study may be used to examine the shaft of the motor at any moment.

As a result, it may be used to estimate the maximum torque that the machinery can withstand. Motor beginning research is required to determine whether or not an electric motor can start and accelerate effectively, as well as how starting affects the machine’s voltage.  

CareLabs’ skilled technicians can perform motor start tests so you can learn more about the effectiveness of your electric motor. Motor start analysis is available in all major cities, including Stockholm, Gothenburg, Malmo, and Uppsala. Please contact us as soon as possible to book your motor starting study or to get a price.

title: Power Quality Analysis
bodyHTML:
• Home
  • About Us
  • Our Services
    • Arc Flash Study
    • Harmonic Study and Analysis
    • Power Quality Analysis
    • Power System Study and Analysis
    • Motor Start Analysis
  • Contact Us

Power Quality Analysis

CareLabs examines the quality of the power for businesses in Sweden. Our goal is to prevent your electrical appliances, equipment, and systems from malfunctioning as a result of erratic power sources.

Enquire
Contact Us

Many organizations and an increasing number of individual users are deploying energy-sensitive data collection, monitoring, and instrumentation systems. Despite the fact that a wide range of voltage changes might occur with distributed energy, few electrical devices are built to handle them. The majority of electrical equipment is designed to handle certain supply variations. Experts from CareLabs are available to assist with problems with power quality, such as equipment installation, control, protection, and power conditioning.   

Power Quality Analysis Serves the Following Purposes:
  • The method of locating problems in an electrical system or a load.
  • Examining the workplace will help simulate techniques for solving issues with power quality.
  • Assessing the load equipment’s functionality and performance
  • The performance and power factor of the machine should be improved.
  • Don’t expend excessive energy.
  • To prevent things from breaking down and dying.
  • Eliminating power-decreasing harmonics, spikes, and transients.
  • Stop frozen PLCs and voltage/frequency spikes.
  • To keep hot transformers, uncomfortable journeys, and bursting capacitors at bay.
  • You should drastically reduce your utility costs.
  • Lengthen the usable life of the electrical network and its parts.
  • Make sure the electrical equipment can tolerate changes by doing an inspection.
Sweden Needs to Perform an Evaluation of the Quality of Its Power:

Every electrical network occasionally experiences power outages, which occur more often during storms, natural disasters, or incidents that destroy power lines.

It is rather common to change the electrical energy source. Changes, interruptions, and distortions in the way electricity is distributed across an electrical network can be divided into three groups:

1. A frequency deviation occurs when a frequency departs from its natural frequency range.

2.Voltage disruptions occur when the voltage’s amplitude exceeds the acceptable range while the voltage’s shape remains unchanged.

2.1Short-term fluctuations include voltage dips and spikes that last up to a minute.

2.2 Under- and over-voltage conditions are examples of longer-lasting variations.

2.3 Unbalanced voltage: When the load is not distributed uniformly across the phases, the equipment overheats and this situation results.

2.4 Voltage difference between the neutral and ground

3. Distortions, which happen when the voltage’s form is not even or smooth, cause disturbances.

3.1 Harmonics and inter-harmonics in electrical equipment cause overheating, power loss, inefficient power use, and resonance problems.

3.2 Transients that are short-lived and last less than a tenth of a second have the potential to damage switch contact surfaces, impair system functionality, and precipitate the failure of motor drives and control systems.

What Is the Process of Power Quality Analysis?

Finding different power quality concerns requires using a variety of strategies. These methods consist of:

  • Potential harmonic challenges are identified and forecasted in harmonic analysis utilizing computer algorithms and reduction techniques.
  • Analysis of Transients and Surges: This inquiry will identify transients and surges.
  • This study would track the magnitude and course of the voltage as well as its transient peaks and dips.
  • The purpose of this investigation is to determine the ideal reactive power at the distribution and load ends.
  • Captive power would be evaluated and coordinated to fulfil demand while using less fuel and energy.
  • Load Flow Study: This study projects power flow magnitudes, power factors, voltage levels, and losses in system branches based on operating conditions.

CareLabs is committed to offering the highest quality service in order to increase the dependability and caliber of its clients’ electrical systems. We provide power quality diagnostic services in significant cities including Stockholm, Gothenburg, Malmo, and Uppsala. Contact us right now to set up a power quality study or to receive a quote.

bodyText:
Home
About Us
Our Services
Contact Us
Power Quality Analysis

CareLabs examines the quality of the power for businesses in Sweden. Our goal is to prevent your electrical appliances, equipment, and systems from malfunctioning as a result of erratic power sources.

Enquire
Contact Us

Many organizations and an increasing number of individual users are deploying energy-sensitive data collection, monitoring, and instrumentation systems. Despite the fact that a wide range of voltage changes might occur with distributed energy, few electrical devices are built to handle them. The majority of electrical equipment is designed to handle certain supply variations. Experts from CareLabs are available to assist with problems with power quality, such as equipment installation, control, protection, and power conditioning.   

Power Quality Analysis Serves the Following Purposes:
The method of locating problems in an electrical system or a load.
Examining the workplace will help simulate techniques for solving issues with power quality.
Assessing the load equipment’s functionality and performance
The performance and power factor of the machine should be improved.
Don’t expend excessive energy.
To prevent things from breaking down and dying.
Eliminating power-decreasing harmonics, spikes, and transients.
Stop frozen PLCs and voltage/frequency spikes.
To keep hot transformers, uncomfortable journeys, and bursting capacitors at bay.
You should drastically reduce your utility costs.
Lengthen the usable life of the electrical network and its parts.
Make sure the electrical equipment can tolerate changes by doing an inspection.
Sweden Needs to Perform an Evaluation of the Quality of Its Power:

Every electrical network occasionally experiences power outages, which occur more often during storms, natural disasters, or incidents that destroy power lines.

It is rather common to change the electrical energy source. Changes, interruptions, and distortions in the way electricity is distributed across an electrical network can be divided into three groups:

1. A frequency deviation occurs when a frequency departs from its natural frequency range.

2.Voltage disruptions occur when the voltage’s amplitude exceeds the acceptable range while the voltage’s shape remains unchanged.

2.1Short-term fluctuations include voltage dips and spikes that last up to a minute.

2.2 Under- and over-voltage conditions are examples of longer-lasting variations.

2.3 Unbalanced voltage: When the load is not distributed uniformly across the phases, the equipment overheats and this situation results.

2.4 Voltage difference between the neutral and ground

3. Distortions, which happen when the voltage’s form is not even or smooth, cause disturbances.

3.1 Harmonics and inter-harmonics in electrical equipment cause overheating, power loss, inefficient power use, and resonance problems.

3.2 Transients that are short-lived and last less than a tenth of a second have the potential to damage switch contact surfaces, impair system functionality, and precipitate the failure of motor drives and control systems.

What Is the Process of Power Quality Analysis?

Finding different power quality concerns requires using a variety of strategies. These methods consist of:

Potential harmonic challenges are identified and forecasted in harmonic analysis utilizing computer algorithms and reduction techniques.
Analysis of Transients and Surges: This inquiry will identify transients and surges.
This study would track the magnitude and course of the voltage as well as its transient peaks and dips.
The purpose of this investigation is to determine the ideal reactive power at the distribution and load ends.
Captive power would be evaluated and coordinated to fulfil demand while using less fuel and energy.
Load Flow Study: This study projects power flow magnitudes, power factors, voltage levels, and losses in system branches based on operating conditions.

CareLabs is committed to offering the highest quality service in order to increase the dependability and caliber of its clients’ electrical systems. We provide power quality diagnostic services in significant cities including Stockholm, Gothenburg, Malmo, and Uppsala. Contact us right now to set up a power quality study or to receive a quote.

title: Power System Study and Analysis
bodyHTML:
• Home
  • About Us
  • Our Services
    • Arc Flash Study
    • Harmonic Study and Analysis
    • Power Quality Analysis
    • Power System Study and Analysis
    • Motor Start Analysis
  • Contact Us

Power System Study and Analysis

CareLabs offers a variety of electrical installation services, including simulations for electrical system design, electrical safety inspections, and power quality analysis studies.

Enquire
Contact Us

Our team of electrical experts performs load flow and short circuit studies, protective device coordination studies, harmonic emission and filter design studies, transient analysis, and network stability studies using ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program), the best tool for power system transient analysis. It enables us to interact directly with our customers in order to develop and provide the finest power compensation and solutions.   

What Is the Purpose of Research on Power System Analysis?

Every industrial or commercial building must have a dependable, regular, and well-maintained electrical system. Even a brief electrical outage can cause significant problems, such as prolonged downtime, vital equipment damage, and expensive repairs. Regular inspections of your building’s power system may be performed to ensure its dependability, safety, and efficiency under both predicted and unforeseen conditions.

At each stage of the design, installation, and operation of a power system, a detailed analysis is required. Using the ETAP program, we will explore and evaluate the power system to discover how it will operate both regularly and in the event that a problem arises unexpectedly. Engineering research has been analyzed to determine how an electric power system responds to transient, dynamic, and static events over a variety of periods.   

Numerous investigations may be conducted to ascertain the condition of the electrical system at your location. It might be included in a study that examines the power system. A couple of these examples are as follows:   

  • The circuit interrupter
  • Establishing a security plan
  • Load movement
  • Constantly changing ratings
  • Investigating arc flash
  • What is the power’s efficiency?
  • Harmonics analysis
  • Launching the vehicle
  • Earth science investigation

It helps companies establish regulations for plant operations, such the electrical safety program. These studies could demonstrate that certain actions are reliable and secure.  

A Load Flow Analysis  

Utilizing load flow studies, you may ascertain the voltages and currents in various network segments before selecting the equipment that is rated for those voltages and currents. With the help of ETAP’s load flow function, you may learn about a power system’s voltages, currents, power consumption, and power factor.   

Short-circuit Investigations:

In order to understand how a power system works when there is a problem, short-circuit studies are necessary. Investigating short circuits manually is a lot of work, but software like ETAP can do it much more quickly and accurately. In 3-phase, L-G, L-L, and L-L-G distribution systems, ETAP is used to simulate system protection devices and determine the implications of distribution system failures.   

Analysis of Transients:

Transient study assesses the size and shape of overvoltage and current spikes brought on by lightning strikes and switching activity. Based on the results, you may choose how much insulation and surge protection you require.   

Investigating Protection Coordination:

This investigation attempts to validate the proper operation of your system’s security components. It proves they are the right size and shape for the item they are defending.

The phases in our research method for power system analysis are typically as follows:

  • Data Collection Methods
  • Data evaluation and client consultations
  • Obtaining equipment details
  • An easy one-line graph
  • ETAP power system modelling
  • Verify the load flow and short circuit.
  • Consider the security coordinating measures.
  • A review of arc flash
  • A detailed examination of the research report
  • Evaluation and management of risks
  • Project management
The Components of the Carelabs Power System Analysis Are as Follows:
  • Opportunities to cut energy costs and use.
  • Reports that identify issues.
  • Suggestions for solutions
  • Information about energy utilization.
  • Participate in the reduction of harm and injuries.
  • The effects of harmonics and transients

In our power system analysis studies, we assess the electrical system of the customer and make recommendations for enhancing its performance and ensuring a stable supply of electricity. In addition to Stockholm, Gothenburg, Malmo, and Uppsala, we evaluate and investigate the energy networks of other significant cities. Contact us right away to set up an evaluation and study of your power system or to get a quote.
